description: Baseline. 3/3 solved but 2/3 timed out at 120s before `(turn-answer! …)`, so their iter counts are unknown and got penalty 30 each. Real signal we have: task 5 needed 12 iterations and ~$0.030. Score is iter-count-sum, lower is better.

asi.primary_metric: iter_score = sum across tasks of (iterations OR FAIL_PENALTY=30 if no iter count)
asi.key_problem: 2/3 tasks hit 120s timeout. GLM-5.1 burns many iterations on slurp/spit/patch mistakes before turn-answer!. Next: count iters from trace stream so timeouts don't blind us; raise per-task timeout to 240s.
user_concerns_added: antifit: revalidate every keep on a different rotation slice
metric_clarity: turns×iters sum example (3+5=8) matches iter_score
context_optimization: include mean_prompt_chars + total_tokens; treat context inflation >50% as keep-blocker

description: Mixed workload baseline (2 × 4Clojure + 1 × filewrite). iter_score=20 across 3 tasks. mean_iter_pass=6.67, max=12. No timeouts. Established new bar with block_errors / z_patch_share / ext_prompt_chars metrics now wired in.

asi.headroom_for_optim: foundation_prompt_chars=8303 dominates; system 3429 next; user 1390 is irreducible without help. Cutting the foundation/z extension prompts is the biggest context lever.
next_targets: shrink foundation `:ext/prompt` body without breaking existing tool dispatch / tests
shrink CORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (gate language is verbose; can compress)
reduce block_errors by guiding model to v/patch over slurp/spit in user prompt
encourage z/patch over v/patch when editing .clj files (z_patch_share metric tracks this)
asi.block_errors_baseline: not measured before this commit; runner now captures it. Re-baselining will set the bar.
asi.z_patch_share_baseline: not measured; baseline metric files don't have it yet because run preceded the metric. First post-commit run sets it.

description: Fixed-workload baseline. Same 3 tasks every iter (4Clojure #5, #15, filewrite fw-005). iter_score=39 (no timeouts; pass_count=3). This is the first run where iter_score is actually comparable across iterations.

asi.hypothesis: anchor a comparable baseline with FIXED_WORKLOAD
key_findings: #15 'Double Down' now takes 23 iters — unexpectedly high; model loops on the '__' textual substitution
v_patch_calls=3, z_patch_calls=0 — model uses v/patch exclusively; z/patch share metric currently 0 even on .clj edits
block_errors=4 — reduced from the rotating-workload baseline (9); the fixed tasks happen to be cleaner shapes for the model
ext_prompt_chars=12410 is the dominant context floor; user_prompt_mean=1409
next_targets: lower svar TTFT/idle to fail fast on stuck provider calls (will pair with the next run)
tighten foundation/z extension prompts to shrink ext_prompt_chars
audit gate language in CORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT for compressibility

description: svar TTFT=10s + idle=10s + dropped virtual threads. pass dropped 3 → 2 (task 4Clojure #15 failed at iter 1 with svar.core/http-error — TTFT fired during legitimate reasoning startup). iter_score 39 → 59, block_errors 4 → 6.

asi.hypothesis: 10s TTFT/idle would fail fast on stuck calls without breaking real ones
asi.result: regression: 10s caught real reasoning startups, not just hangs

per_task.4clojure-15: FAIL 1 iter 22s wall - TTFT/idle fired during reasoning
per_task.filewrite-fw-005: PASS 19 iters 173s (was 4 iters 60s - 5x worse, likely many retries on transient timeouts)

asi.rollback_reason: pass_count dropped, iter_score got worse on the same fixed workload
asi.next_action_hint: Bump svar TTFT to 20-30s and idle to 30-45s. glm-5.1 cold-start headers sometimes >10s; legitimate reasoning streams can pause briefly. Virtual-thread removal stays (orthogonal).
asi.svar_changes_to_partial_revert: TTFT 10000 -> 30000, idle 10000 -> 45000; keep cached thread pool. Will apply in ../svar manually since vis discard doesn't touch the sibling repo.
